creator: swell.k@gmail.com
text:
Philip M. Gollucci <pgollucci@FreeBSD.org> writes:

> Request feedback why is this bad?

makeinfo(1) version banner should be kept inside INFO file, not
propagated into DIR file. DIR file is created and maintained by
install-info(1). And lang/gawk is the only[1] port that spams DIR file.

But what's worse, the banner is not removed upon deinstall because it's
not a valid entry for DIR file. So, you'll end up with smth like

  Network applications
  This is gawkinet.info, produced by makeinfo version 4.11 from
  gawkinet.texi.

  This is gawkinet.info, produced by makeinfo version 4.11 from
  gawkinet.texi.

with no way to figure out which port left this garbage.

OTOH, the practice to remove pregenerated files can be often found in
ports but it's usually to avoid installing splitted INFO files.

[1] other ports that I have installed that don't spam DIR file

        autoconf-2.13.000227_6
        autoconf-2.62
        autoconf-2.67
        automake-1.10.1
        automake-1.11.1
        automake-1.4.6_5
        binutils-2.20.51
        bison-2.4.3,1
        ccache-3.0.1
        emacs-24.0.50.101380,1
        fftw3-3.2.2_1
        flex-2.5.35_4
        gcc-4.5.2.20100902
        gettext-0.18_1
        gmake-3.81_4
        gmp-5.0.1
        gnugrep-2.6.3_3
        gnulib-20100704_1
        gperf-3.0.3
        help2man-1.38.2_1
        libIDL-0.8.14_1
        libcheck-0.9.8
        libffi-3.0.9
        libidn-1.15_1
        libtool-2.2.8
        m4-1.4.14_1,1
        mpc-0.8.2
        mpfr-3.0.0
        msmtp-1.4.21
        sbcl-1.0.42.0
        screen-devel+wrp-4.1.0.20100622
        stumpwm-0.9.7
        texi2html-1.82,1
        zsh-4.3.10_3
